entry.title: Posting rejected (UPDATE_REJECT).Order 104XXXX could not be saved and Message No.COIS008
Re: Asset Accounting - What is ECC t-code ABSK in S/4Hana...
entry.content:
On my oldest ECC systems (but not 20 years old) these transaction types were related to

  • 200 - Retirement without revenue
  • 210 - Retirement with revenue

So SAP transactions such as 

  • F-92 - Retirement With Customer
  • ABAO Asset Sale Without Customer
  • ABAV Asset Retirement by Scrapping

Would relate to S/4HANA Cloud App id such as F7107 - Post Asset Retirement (Feel free to look for any Post Asset App id from the link.

But I did not found any ABSK transaction (sorry)

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: we need support, please... we cant enter in application for 3 days. I create new databases and web applications for tests.. and the loggin not work anymore ( Email & password authentication (Firebase Auth))

entry.title: SAP BUILD importing .mtar file error
entry.content:
Hello,

We changed our plan from free to standard and we are importing our build apps projects. I am experiencing this error for 3 out of 6 projects that we have. I double checked and the project is not existing. I also tried creating a new project with the same name and I was able to create it and delete it.

I found one solution in google, but I don't have access to this link.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-knowledge-base/workaround-for-error-importing-projects-in-sap-build-apps/ta-p/220085

Can anyone know what can we do to upload our projects? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS issue with using replace : Number of positions > maximum number (1333) for data type
entry.content:
As stated above, the command works as described. But that's not how we use the command: especially in the typical usage to replace one occurences.

But, there's one easy way to solve the problem: 

define view entity zzlk_vb_message 
as select from t100
{
  replace(
    replace(
      t100.text,
      cast( '&1' as abap.sstring(50)),
        'Something_to_insert'
      ),
      cast( '&2' as abap.sstring(50) ),
      'Something_to_insert'
  ) as message 
}

 With this simple cast the syntax check allows this combination and the result is as expected.

entry.title: Issue creating storage bins via LS01N – Missing LGTYP and LGBER values
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently facing an issue while trying to create new storage bins using transaction LS01N.

The problem is that the fields Storage Type (LGTYP) and Storage Section (LGBER) are missing or not maintained – they show up empty, and I can’t proceed.

I assume these values are supposed to be predefined in the system, but it looks like they’re not.

 

My questions are:

1. Where and how can I maintain or create Storage Types (LGTYP) and Storage Sections (LGBER)?

2. What are the relevant transactions or SPRO paths to configure this?

3. Are there any important dependencies or things to watch out for when setting them up?

Would really appreciate a step-by-step guide or any advice from someone who's done this before. 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Historical balances
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, once you migrate historical financial data (such as opening balances or historical balances) using the "Migrate Your Data" app, these are stored as technical documents, and they cannot be cancelled or reversed in the traditional sense (i.e., no FI document is posted that can be reversed with FB08 or similar).

This is standard system behavior, and you're correct in observing that « the system only generates a technical document that cannot be cancelled ».

 

Here’s some options you can study on :

Option 1: No Deletion Tool Available in Public Cloud

SAP does not provide a standard tool in S/4HANA Public Cloud to delete migrated data. This restriction ensures data consistency and aligns with audit-compliant practices.

 

Option 2: Reset the Migration in Test System via SAP Support

If you're in a test client or test system, and you need to reset migrated data, the standard approach is:

Create an SAP incident and request SAP to reset the migration content for the relevant object (e.g., historical balances, open items, etc.).

This is the only supported method for clean-up, especially if the technical objects are already created and cannot be deleted manually.

What you’ll need to provide:

  • System ID / client
  • Migration object used (e.g., "FI – Historical G/L Balances")
  • Confirmation that it is not a production system
  • Reason for reset (e.g., testing error, incorrect balances, testing re-migration)

 

Option 3: Overwrite Balances with New Migration

If the reset is not possible or feasible, you can consider re-uploading the balances with reversed or corrected values via a new migration object run.

Example:

  • Original upload: +100 USD to GL account
  • New upload: -100 USD to same GL account → Net effect = 0

However, this approach:

  • Creates additional technical entries (it doesn't delete or overwrite the old one),
  • May not be suitable for full clean-up in testing scenarios.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How can I change growing to scroll in a Fiori element...
entry.content:
ok, possible to include a custom section in Object Page Noel?

And inside that a smart table (scrollable by default)

  <SmartTable id="mySmartTable"
              entitySet="ProductSet"
              tableType="Table"
              useVariantManagement="true"
              useTablePersonalisation="true"
              useExportToExcel="true"
              header="Products"
              smartFilterId="SmartFilterBar"
              demandPopin="false"
              enableAutoBinding="true">
  </SmartTable>

entry.title: Re: EWM Physical Inventory Error : Enter a Warehouse Numb...

entry.title: In the Product Description Page, the title attribute on the cx-media is being over ridden by alt
entry.content:
I have upgraded to Spartacus 2211.32 and noticed that the "title" tag on cx-media no longer works. I have created a custom "productImageComponent" by extended the existing "ProductImageZoomProductImagesComponent" and using the "title" attribute on the cx-media. But the title is not visible when I hover on the image. Seems like its being over ridden by the "alt" attribute.

Seems like OOTB, alt and title are set to same value. Is there a way to over ride this?

<img
      [loading]="loading"
      [alt]="media.alt"
      [title]="media.alt"
      [src]="media.src"
      [attr.role]="media.role"
      (load)="loadHandler()"
      (error)="errorHandler()"
    />

entry.title: EWM Physical Inventory Error : Enter a Warehouse Number Message no. /SCWM/LC1194
entry.content: Hi gurus, I am getting an error while performing a physical inventory Please help me out of this.

entry.title: Agentic Testing and Simulation with Int4 Suite's SAP Business Knowledge Graph
entry.content:
Intro 

Have you ever wondered why simulation and test automation software is not accessible in a easy way to everyone for SAP projects? Now with Agentic Testing and Simulation based on Int4 Suite's SAP Business Knowledge Graph it's no longer the case with Int4 Suite. You can use your enterprise AI and to simulate any 3rd party test system and run any automated SAP Business process test defined in Int4 Suite.

Why use Agents and AI for Testing and Simulation of SAP applications?

The Int4 Suite stores millions of historical electronic messages (test cases) pulled from various integration platforms and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC backends using Int4 Suite Robotic Crawler, as shown in the figure below.

These messages can be grouped into folders and executed across development and test environments. However, this traditional approach comes with two major challenges:

a) Finding the Right Test Cases - Even with our powerful Test Case Analyzer, searching through millions of test messages can be overwhelming. Imagine needing to find all messages related to incoming sales orders for:

Customer: 5002334

Materials: T-DK0001 and B-234234

Even with perfectly structured folders, locating the right data might still take several minutes. Multiply that effort across teams and use cases, it adds up.

b) Accessibility and Democratization of Testing - At Int4, we champion citizen testing. Testing and simulation shouldn’t be limited to users with automation expertise. Anyone should be able to run tests, manual or automated, easily and intuitively. That’s why Int4 Suite isn’t licensed by the number of users. We believe in unrestricted access for all who need to test SAP-integrated business processes.

How Agentic Testing and Simulation Solves These Challenges?

With Agentic Testing and Simulation built on Int4 Suite’s SAP Business Knowledge Graph, we can either eliminate or significantly reduce the two main pain points above:

a) Smarter Test Case Discovery - by combining:

  • Historical messages,

  • Master data,

  • And entity relationships,

our intelligent agents enable users to perform semantic and business-context-based searches across all test cases. You can now search using natural language or business-specific criteria and execute relevant tests instantly similarly as shown in Figure below. 

b) AI-Driven Chat for Testing - thanks to enterprise AI and chat-based interfaces, anyone, from any SAP S/4HANA or SAP BTP project, can now access and use testing features without needing to understand the underlying software mechanics.

This brings simulation and automated testing closer to business analysts, project managers, and functional consultants, not just developers or testers.

Why Int4 Suite’s SAP Business Knowledge Graph is a Game Changer?

Having access to millions of test messages is powerful. But being able to query those test cases from a business perspective makes it revolutionary.

Instead of filtering only by material numbers or quantities, imagine asking:

  • Pharmaceutical industry:
    "Show me test cases of sales orders containing materials flagged as high-risk."

  • FMCG industry:
    "Find sales orders from partners where deliveries were most delayed in the last 3–6 months—especially when it led to lost priority unloading slots."

To enable these kinds of queries, the Business Knowledge Graph links test data with master data and maps relationships between them—unlocking far more intuitive, business-relevant insights.

Where to Learn More About Int4 Suite?

👉Visit our SAP Learning Course - Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization

entry.title: WFA on Hana Succession Metric: Applicable Succession Positions Count
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 :

We have a query regarding the WFA Succession measure Applicable Succession Positions. The client is using a position-based nomination method for Succession.

According to the specification, the Applicable Succession Positions measure should count the number of positions at the end of the reporting period. However, it appears to be counting the number of unique Successor IDs instead.

We noticed this during our review of the WFA Drill to Detail. The data provided focuses solely on Successor details, rather than on active positions. For example, if a department has 100 active positions but only 20 successors (with unique position IDs), the measure returns a value of 20.

Given the limited scope of the current measure, we wanted to understand whether the measure’s definition could be clearer or whether the code itself needs to be adjusted to better support position-based nominations.

How would we tweak this code to reflect the expected count—i.e. the number of positions rather than count of successors’ positions?

Thank you.

Best regards,

Zaklin Perosa

@mikehoekstra1

entry.title: Re: Firebase integration not working with SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
My conclusion is the component problem: "LogicEmail & password authentication (Firebase Auth)" . I create a new /clean test project just the 1 screen: login - default components - just change the connections / databases.. all the same response: "the user / password is incorrect. " . 2 days ago, all databases connected ... now.. none.
aditional information - I test one old version (3 weeks ago) published in firebase.. and this version work the login.. but.. I compiled with the last version.. and now, dont work anymore.

So. for me.. we have one problem in this actual version.

entry.title: Interactive Fragmented/Adobe Forms using Adobe Live Cycle
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Developed fragmented adobe forms using Adobe Live Cycle designer which is linked to RAP model at the backend, currently stuck with one requirement to make the fragmented form interactive as business wants checkboxes to be interactive and should be able to alter checkbox data and save in db the changes from front end. We are currently displaying the adobe form using UI5 front end and using pdf container just to display form.

Can anyone help me making the adobe form/fragmented forms interactive. Any suggestion guiding on the same is really appreciated.

Thanks & Best Regards!!

entry.title: Re: Rounding Logic Documentation: Rounding from 0.5 in SAP ABAP
Event Based Product Costing & Actual Overhead Application
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I do have one question.

In Event Based Product Costing, Actual Overheads are getting charged immediately on to Production Order if we have maintained Actual OH Rate / Percentage in Costing Sheet OH Rates.

Where as in Actual Business Condition, Determination of Actual OH Rate is only possible after Period End Closing.  How to handle this?

How to allocate actual incurred overheads on to product during Actual Costing??

I have gone through Actual Costing 33Q, Event Based Product Costing (3F0) Processes, but I could not find the answer.  

Please guide me in this scenario.

Thanks in Advance

Nitin

BR, Timo Kapanen

entry.title: Out-of-the-Box Document Grounding in SAP AI Core: RAG with SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Introduction

As enterprises adopt large language models (LLMs), grounding responses in trusted sources is essential to ensure accuracy and contextual relevance. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses this need by dynamically retrieving relevant content to guide model outputs.
SAP AI Core simplifies this process through its document grounding capability, which allows developers to enhance LLMs with enterprise-specific knowledge. Most notably, help.sap.com is already grounded by default—meaning you can immediately retrieve content from SAP’s official product documentation without any setup. In addition, SAP AI Core supports integration with custom data repositories such as SharePoint, AWS S3, and Metadata, providing flexibility for broader use cases.

This blog focuses on testing retrieval from help.sap.com, showcasing how easy it is to build a RAG workflow using out-of-the-box SAP content. Let’s dive in.

Prerequisite

  • Subscription to SAP AI Core (Extended plan)

Restrictions (as of this blog publication):

  • SAP AI Core free tier is only available in SAP BTP free tier, not SAP BTP trial.
  • The Extended Plan for SAP AI Core is only available with CPEA or BTPEA entitlement. You can find more details about pricing and service plans in the Discovery Center here.
  • Not all SAP product documentation is indexed yet within the Help Portal data repository. However, most major SAP solutions are already covered. If you notice occasional gaps or less relevant retrievals, keep in mind that the index is continuously improving over time.

Initial Setup

Step 1: Complete foundational setup of SAP AI Core instance

You can refer to this help documentation that already provide step by step guides here (Enabling the service in Cloud Foundry). Ensure that you choose the extended plan in order to use generative AI capabilities.

Step 2: Download and import Postman collection

To interact with SAP AI Core programmatically—whether for workflow orchestration, document grounding setup, or managing resources—developers can leverage the prebuilt Postman collection available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

You can refer to this help documentation (Using a 3rd Party API Platform) on the procedure to download JSON collection from SAP Business Accelerator Hub, and import into Postman. In this blog, you will need to download & import these OpenAPI JSON collections:

  • SAP AI Core - AI Core
  • SAP AI Core - Grounding
  • SAP AI Core - Orchestration


Alternatively, you’re free to use your preferred API platform tool—such as Bruno, cURL, or others—based on your workflow and tooling preferences. In this blog, I will be using Postman for demonstration purposes.

Quick Start

REST APIs that we need for this blog are as follows:

  • /SAP AI Core /AI Core /Resource Group > POST /admin/resourceGroups
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > GET /retrieval/dataRepositories
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > POST /retrieval/search
  • /SAP AI Core /Orchestration /Completion > POST /completion
Step 1: Create a resource group for document grounding

Before we begin, we a resource group that segment our document grounding workloads. Use the following API endpoint to create a resource group: POST /admin/resourceGroups. Include the following JSON payload in the request body. Make sure to add the label key "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding" with the value "true"—this is required to enable document grounding capabilities within the resource group

{
  "resourceGroupId": "<input resource group id here>",
  "labels": [
    {
      "key": "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding",
      "value": "true"
    }
  ]
}

In my example, I'm using 'grounding' as my resource group id, but it can be any unique name / id of your choice.

Step 2: List all data repository objects (Optional)

In case you're interested to see all data repository objects that are available in your AI Core resource group, you can use this API: GET /retrieval/dataRepositories

As shown in the response, a default repository named 'help.sap.com' is already provisioned. This built-in integration allows you to perform RAG directly on official SAP Help documentation—enabling immediate access to curated enterprise knowledge without additional setup.

To perform get all data repository objects, remember to update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request headers. You should see the output below; for my case, I have an additional data repository object from SharePoint, thus another entry showing 'vector' in addition to 'help.sap.com'.



Step 3: Retrieval search via Vector (Approach #1)

There are 2 ways to query grounded documents from data repositories be it from data repository type 'vector' or type 'help.sap.com'. One of the way is using vector retrieval search. This API queries the connected data repository and returns the most relevant content chunks based on the user-provided query: POST /retrieval/search.

To perform retrieval search on 'help.sap.com' data repository, same as above update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, and use this JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "query": "",
  "filters": [
    {
      "id": "filter1",
      "dataRepositoryType": "help.sap.com",
      "dataRepositories": [
        "*"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

In my example, I want to know the official migration guide for SAP PI/PO, thus entering this question "show me migration guide for SAP Process Orchestration" in "query".

Retrieval search results as follows:



Step 4: Retrieval search via Orchestration (Approach #2)

This is another way to query grounded documents via orchestration service. What it does it integrates document grounding configurations and filters to provide precise results. With the orchestration service, you can interact with LLMs from multiple providers through a unified interface, without changing your application code. Switching models is as simple as updating a configuration parameter, making it easy to compare performance or adapt to different requirements. It also supports advanced features like prompt templating, content filtering, data masking, and grounding for secure and context-aware AI integration.

To get started, you’ll need to create at least one orchestration deployment—or use the default deployment within your resource group. If no deployments are found, please refer to this tutorial for guidance tutorial.

To perform retrieval search using orchestraction service with 'help.sap.com' as data repository source, make sure that you update the "deploymentId" URL path variable and update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, then use this example JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "orchestration_config": {
    "module_configurations": {
      "grounding_module_config": {
        "type": "document_grounding_service",
        "config": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "id": "filter1",
              "data_repositories": [
                "*"
              ],
              "search_config": {
                "max_chunk_count": 5
              },
              "data_repository_type": "help.sap.com"
            }
          ],
          "input_params": [
            "groundingRequest"
          ],
          "output_param": "groundingOutput"
        }
      },
      "llm_module_config": {
        "model_name": "",
        "model_params": {},
        "model_version": ""
      },
      "templating_module_config": {
        "template": [
          {
            "role": "user",
            "content": ""
          }
        ],
        "defaults": {}
      },
      "filtering_module_config": {
        "input": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "output": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "input_params": {
    "groundingRequest": ""
  }
}

Orchestration service result as follows:

Essentially what I need is the 'message' > 'content' from 'orchestration_result'. If you want to see the complete orchestration schema, refer to this link.

Conclusion

As shown above, the retrieval search returns highly relevant results for the given query—including document chunks closely aligned with the intended topic. This significantly reduces the time spent manually searching through documentation, making the process of grounding large language models both efficient and precise. As SAP continues to integrate AI into its business technology ecosystem, capabilities like these will play a key role in delivering intelligent, reliable enterprise applications.

For more information on SAP AI Core

  • Exchange knowledge: SAP Community
  • Learn more: Help Portal | Roadmap | Orchestration
  • Explore: Product Information

Special thanks to product experts that provide me advise during my testing: @Sumedha_Garg @gesine_stanienda . 
Also do check out other blog from my colleague @rachel_gan  on "Setting up SAP Document Grounding for Chat Apps" with SharePoint as data repository source.

I would really appreciate any guidance or insights from those with experience — particularly on which European countries offer the best opportunities for a career in SAP, and how many years of experience would be ideal before making the move. Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Exchange Rate Difference Line with Zero Amount Preven...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

 

Have you checked the SAP note below ?

2539550 - More gain/loss lines generated with amount 0.00 in LC1 or LC2 when clearing

 

It might be useful to answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Delete Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is not possible
entry.content:
Hi,

Support should be able to help you.

you can also utilize the CIC 

  1. For guidance on how to contact the SAP Customer Interaction Center, please refer to SAP Note 560499– Customer Interaction Center: Hotline - Email - Chat
  2. CIC Hotline à 1 800 677 7271, Option 2
  3. Expert Chat à https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#incident/create (For quick resolutions on new issues!)
  4. Schedule an Expert à https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/sae (For convenient, personalized SAP support on your schedule!)
  5. Customer Portal à https://support.sap.com

There could be some transactions or other data associated with the BP preventing the deletion.

entry.title: Re: EOIO in SAP CI Using JMS - Exclusive Mode
entry.content:
@devang_mandli  Yes I assumed it would be there but then I checked its not there. The only option is to maintain a counter in a datastore with datastore id as "1" that holds the message id of the JMS message and updates a counter if its already there in data store, if its not there then overwrites the existing entry with a counter 0 and with the new message id. 

But honestly this is just like how the inventor of the first car forgot to brakes in it.

entry.title: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relevant to cost accounting Message no. KI235
entry.content:
We are running an asset depreciation job in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and receiving the following error:

Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relevant to cost accounting
Message no. KI235

The asset in question already has a cost center assigned in the asset master data, and we do not want to override or use any other cost center.

Could you please help clarify why the system is still requiring a separate CO assignment during depreciation, even though it’s already maintained in the asset master?
Also, is there a way to ensure the system always pulls the cost center from the asset master automatically during depreciation?

We want to avoid maintaining default account assignments or substitutions for this scenario, since the correct cost center is already maintained on the asset.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAP TM Complete Configuration Guide
entry.content: Hi, Can anyone share the SAP TM complete configuration guide to my mail id - cska29031991@gmail.com

entry.title: Unable to find the scope item IDs for SAP PS for SAP cloud private 2023-FSP02
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are using SAP Cloud Private Edition 2023-FSP02 for our customer. I need the SAP PS Customer Project and Capital Project scope item IDs to link in cloud ALM. I downloaded the master BPML from SAP portal and it has PS10 and PS11 respectively for both the project types but there's no documentation maintained on the SAP portal under these scope IDs.

Can I use 1NT-Project Control Finance and BNA-Period end closing for these project types? Please suggest the solution as I need to finalize the scope item IDs as soon as possible.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Unable to Create/Post STO Purchase order Using BO/API
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We're trying to post a STO Purchase Order using Standard API & same we try with business object, But we get a error of (MessageClass='APPL_MM_PUR_PO',MessageNumber='065'  Short Text = Use a supported purchase order item category for item &. See long text.) 

please help with this to find out the way to resolve this.

 

Link - https://my418769-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurchaseOrder

Payload - 
{
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "PurchaseOrderType": "UD",
    "Supplier": "1004",
    "PurchasingOrganization": "1000",
    "PurchasingGroup": "003",
    "_PurchaseOrderItem": [
        {
            "Plant": "1007",
            "OrderQuantity": 7,
            "NetPriceAmount": 0,
            "NetPriceQuantity": 1,
            "PurchaseOrderItemCategory": "7",
            "TaxCode": "2E",
            "DocumentCurrency" : "",
            "OrderPriceUnit" : "",
            "PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit" : " "
           
        }
    ]
}

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Thank you @timokapanen help will be much appreciated.

entry.title: BW Query statistics not available for CDS-based Queries on transient providers in S/4 HANA system
entry.content:
Hi,

I have built BW Queries on top of CDS Views exposed as transient providers in the S/4HANA system. However, I am unable to find the statistics for these queries.

I have enabled statistics by setting it to 'ON' in Transaction RSDDSTAT, but the data is still not being captured in the underlying table RSDDSTAT_OLAP.

Could you please help me with how to capture statistics for BW Queries in S/4HANA?

Thanks,

K Srinivas

entry.title: Purchase Order forms
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

 

Is it possible to add condition record lines in purchase order form in line items details 

eg. If we purchase metals like silver copper prices  in condition record can we make display of it in po print for supplier to know how many meters and what is the market price as of ordering and then net amount to be dispalyed and leads highly appreciated .

expected these prices to be displayed in Purchase order .

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-Ons Samples Preview
entry.content:
Hi, thank you for the useful information - it was very helpful! I tried using the Datepicker as a custom widget SAC, and while the widget itself works, the Builder Panel doesn’t show any options to link it to a data model. As a result, the Datepicker doesn’t actually filter any data. What steps do I need to take to make it work correctly with the data model?

Thanks.

Thanks

Shivang

CUSTOM LOGIC FOR INCOTERMS

we have a custom logic for a company code already that triggers incoterms in PO line items .

However, we wanted to have same functionality for another company code to trigger in PO line item level.

Can we extend same custom logic for another company code or we need to create new logic for new company 

if we can extend, how can we achieve it .

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

The message you get is due to a missing entry for the G/L account 64004000 in the default account assignment configuration that you can find in the SSCUI 102639.

CBC path :

I hope this will help you solve your problem.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content: Hello Jeremy! Yes I understand that the Manage Default Account Assignment could be the solution, but that is not what we want. Why do we have to use a default cost center when there's already one maintained in the fixed asset master data?

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE HADR - database replication limit

Hi @AliSaad,

fro ATP stock, you should use the application Monitor Product Availability which is equivalent of CO09 transaction from SAP GUI. This app should meet the requirements which you mentioned for the Sales Team.

Unfortunately for the EWM-related questions, I am not able to advice.  

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Displaying Currency in Indian Numbering Format
entry.content: In Crystal Reports 2020 there is no Common Tab- Display String in field formatting. What is the solution for CR 2020?

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content:
Dear user,

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, for manual journal entries or accounting postings that aren’t asset-specific transactions, the system doesn’t always derive the cost center from the asset master, even though it’s technically maintained there.

This is due to:

  • The account assignment logic in the standard journal entry app.
  • The lack of direct asset subledger involvement in some postings.
  • The fact that account assignments like cost centers are driven by account determination logic rather than the asset master itself.

Do not hesitate if you need some more details.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to create a OData Service in Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi @Jerry_Lowery ,

In SAP GUI, I can create an OData for a Custom table with CRUD Operations, which I can also use in the Fiori Application. But how do I achieve that in the public cloud?

Thanks,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: NOW RELEASED: Upload and work with external files in SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
entry.content: Many thanks for the Post. With the DMS, will the 8GB restriction still be in place (The primary bottleneck with documentation management in SAP Cloud ALM is the 8 GB data upload limit. Once this limit is reached, SAP charges $3,900 for every additional 3-4 GB of data. Therefore, SAP advises linking existing documentation to SAP documentation in CALM rather than storing it directly) or unlimited upload is allowed?

entry.title: Re: How to have measures in between dimensions in SAC Tab...
entry.content: @Kumar71, I tried taking all the dimensions (which are there in table) as the dimension context. The problem however is it works for a table with small number of columns, my table has 72 columns and if I select all 72, then it shows null in the calculated dimension.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order forms
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, adding condition record lines directly in the Purchase Order (PO) form at line item level - such as displaying detailed condition types and amounts - is not available by default in the standard PO output form.

However, yes, it is technically possible to include them, but it requires a form customization using Adobe LiveCycle Designer and Custom Fields & Logic or Output Management configuration. Here's how it can be done:

 

Options to include condition records in PO Line Items (Form Output)

Option 1 -  Enhance the Form Layout

You need to adapt the Purchase Order Form via "Maintain Form Templates" app:

  • Go to “Maintain Form Templates” app.
  • Find the template: e.g., MM_PUR_PURCHASE_ORDER or similar (based on Output Type).
  • Download and edit in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.
  • In the form context, under each PO item, you can access the pricing conditions if the form interface includes the structure: ITEM_CONDITIONS.

Check: If the field ITEM_CONDITIONS or CONDITIONTABLE is not present, you might need to use Custom Form Templates + CDS view extension.

 

Option 2 - Use Custom CDS View if Necessary

If the standard form does not expose condition details at line item level:

  • Build a Custom CDS View to expose purchase order item condition data.
  • Use data source like I_PurchaseOrderItem and associate with I_PurchasingDocumentCondition.
  • Include fields like:
    • ConditionType
    • ConditionRate
    • ConditionAmount
  • Then extend the data source used in the form output with your custom CDS view.

 

Option 3 - Adapt the Output Determination (optional)

If you're using Output Management with BRF+, ensure the adapted form template is assigned to the correct output type (e.g., PURCHASE_ORDER) and channel (e.g., PDF, Email).

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providing parameter
entry.content: 'Dynamic prepare' supports queries, not stored procedure calls.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order forms

entry.title: Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation - changes?!
entry.content:
Hi, 

We've a customer running a public cloud project at the moment, we'r now in the realize phase and doing config in the system. I'm about to do config related to Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation, following guides and blogposts but it seems like some changes has been made compared to previously related to GL-accounts. When reading: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/advanced-foreign-currency-valuation-in-s-4hana-cloud-cloud-erp/ba-p/13562579 I can see that in the step Define Rules for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation (ID: 105450) when you define Assign G/L Accounts for FSV your about to enter G/L accounts both realized and unrealized accounts:

But, when checking in the same step in the customer CBC there are different headings? 

It seems like the realized has been removed and replaced with Reset XX?
What is the "Reset account" used for? Where can I find the realized account? 

Where is the documentation related to this? 

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: RSAX_BIW_GET_DATA_SIMPLE - The extraction program does not support object
entry.content: Very helful thank you - always make sure in function CASE i_dsource WHEN ' name of datasource' you don't put structure name 🙂

entry.title: Question: Difference between Learning Proxy page vs Learning Proxy in EC.
entry.content:
Hello everyone (Experts),

I'm currently in the middle of a project to implement SuccessFactors Learning, and I've come across something that I'm having trouble understanding.

In Employee Central, when I go to Manage Proxy for a user, I see there's an option to enable "Learning". If I check this box and save, does it mean the person I've granted proxy rights to will be able to access the Learning page on behalf of the user?

I'm asking this because I also noticed that within LMS, there’s a separate option to proxy as a user directly from the Learning platform. So my question is:
Do both proxy options function in the same way, or are there key differences between them?

Taking advantage of this question, when I delegate my tasks to someone else in Employee Central (for example, during my vacation), will that person also be able to access my training in the Learning module?

Thanks in advance for your help!

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors API language
entry.content:
Hi @alexandermenz ,

Thank you for your response. I appreciate your input; however, I believe there might have been a misunderstanding regarding my original question.

I'm specifically seeking a technical solution to force the SAP SuccessFactors API to return localized data in a specified language, independent of the user's language settings. For instance, if I'm accessing the API as a user whose language is set to English (UK), I want to retrieve data—such as country names—localized in French (France) without altering the user's language preference.

I already have a logic in place that checks the user's local settings and selects the appropriate name_ property based on that. However, I'm looking for a method to directly instruct the API to return data in a specific language, such as French, regardless of the user's current language configuration.

If you have insights or suggestions on how to implement this, perhaps by passing a language parameter in the API request or through another method that allows the API to return data in the desired localization, I would greatly appreciate your guidance.

Best regards

Soultan Hatsijev

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors API language
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Energy supplier badenova transforms work order coordination with SAP Fiori Makers boot camp
entry.content:
In September 2024, SAP customer and German energy supplier badenova embarked on a transformative journey to streamline its work order coordination processes. To better manage the persistent operational challenges around customer requests, its subsidiary badenovaNETZE joined the SAP Fiori Makers program for an intensive, hands-on boot camp in Freiburg, Germany.

The boot camp focused on addressing and analyzing the real challenges faced by the company’s work order coordinators to optimize their daily work experience and routines.

The challenge: A fragmented, manual process

Before the boot camp, badenovaNETZE’s work order coordination was a fragmented mix of tools and processes. Coordinators were juggling SAP software, custom programs from SmartService Solutions, and basic applications like Word and Excel to complete their tasks. The status quo resulted in a couple of challenges to address and optimize:

  • Improve user experience: The existing interface was difficult to navigate, leading to user frustration and reduced productivity.
  • Avoid manual processes: Coordinators had to both transfer information across multiple systems and update data manually, making routine tasks time-consuming and prone to errors.
  • Reduce error rate: The repetitive, manual tasks increased the risk of data entry errors, causing delays and additional workload.
  • Increase transparency: With limited visibility into work order statuses, coordinators spent a lot of time tracking down information and monitoring progress.

The solution: A hands-on transformation

The company participated in a four-day SAP Fiori Makers boot camp, bringing together customer end users, IT teams, and partners. The focus of the boot camp was to improve the usability of the work order cockpit by transitioning from manual tasks and workarounds to an SAP app prototype as a tangible starting point. It aimed at implementing process automation, incorporating dashboards, enhancing the user experience, and reducing error rates.

The boot camp provided badenovaNETZE with the tools and an open, agile workshop format needed to tackle these challenges. Based on this close collaboration with the company’s coordinators, the SAP team developed customized solutions that streamlined processes and the final outcome. “The beauty of our boot camps is that we get to know and truly understand the challenges of our end users,” SAP UX Designer Christina Salwitzek summarizes. “Together with the end user and from the beginning, we designed and developed a more efficient solution for this complex workflow.”

Preview of a work order screen at badenova (menu items are in German)

 

The objectives

badenovaNETZE faces operational challenges when managing numerous customer requests in metering, including meter replacement, installation, and removal. Together, the teams set ambitious yet achievable objectives to ensure long-term success. Operational readiness by 2025 was the top priority, with a clear goal of transitioning to an optimized, future-proof system in the first half of the new year.

To support this transition, the boot camp helped increase SAP technology knowledge and development skills within the badenovaNETZE team. The team is now prepared to maintain, adapt, and expand the new solution as their needs evolve. “What surprised me the most is that we actually made such good progress together and that everybody was able to contribute something,” says Paulina Deren, order coordination advisor at badenovaNETZE.

Finally, the team established a prioritized road map—a clear, phased approach designed to guide the customer through each stage of the transformation, enabling a smooth journey from concept to realization. The SAP Fiori Makers team included three UX designers, an SAP Fiori elements front-end developer who brought the visual designs to life, and a backend developer specializing in the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model framework to help ensure robust, scalable functionality. The team was guided by Andreas Spahn, principal technology & innovation manager from the SAP Services team, who moderated the boot camp and facilitated collaboration. “What makes SAP Fiori Makers so special is that we have one problem statement and we have one team to analyze today’s problem with the end user and solve it,” he says. “This involves experts from technology, design, and development.”

The SAP design system is ready to usher in the next evolution of enterprise software
Learn more

The experience

A core element was the creation of a user-centric and future-focused design for a new work order cockpit. The team used design thinking techniques in combination with the capabilities of SAP Fiori, SAP Fiori elements, and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to get started. Being one of Germany’s largest regional suppliers of electricity, natural gas, heat energy, and water, badenova already used SAP BTP for integration scenarios and started using it for application development with this project.

For its intuitive, streamlined interface, the team conducted a thorough feasibility assessment to validate the proposed solution’s technical and operational viability. They then rapidly co-created and prototyped solutions in close collaboration with actual users.

The derived prototype not only simplified complex workflows but also significantly reduced manual tasks, minimized errors, and eliminated data silos. By centralizing all relevant information into a unified system, the team delivered a seamless user experience, initiating the groundwork necessary for full deployment.

The result garnered positive feedback from participants, reflecting the practicality and innovation of the solution. “Most impressive to me was the headway made,” Daniel Oberle, business architect at badenovaNETZE, says. “Without the SAP Fiori Makers boot camp, we would be looking at another half a year to get to where we are just now.”

Real user feedback

To ensure the solution truly resonated with its users, the team conducted three on-site, moderated usability tests using a Figma prototype. Each session included two notetakers capturing live feedback directly within Figma, allowing for immediate insights and adjustments. The initial user stories were visualized using SAP’s Scenes storytelling tool, which enabled the team to quickly create collaborative, visual narratives without the need for drawing skills. These early concepts were then refined with SAP Fiori design stencils in Figma, streamlining the transition from rough ideas to polished prototypes.

The involvement of SAP users during the boot camp was instrumental, providing real-time evaluation through cognitive walkthroughs. “If the user experience fits, employees are happy and enjoy using the software,” Teresa Mayer, UX designer at SAP, says. This hands-on, iterative process helped ensure the final design aligned closely with user expectations and needs.

This example underscores the critical role user research plays in driving successful, error-free, and beneficial product development. To strengthen its user feedback approach even further, SAP introduced the SAP User Research Panel program. This initiative invites users to participate in remote research activities, allowing SAP’s product teams to get feedback along the product life cycle. Users can share their product and design experiences to help shape SAP products to better align with their needs while also getting a preview of new features or products.

SAP Fiori Makers Bootcamp Badenova

Click the button below to load the content from YouTube.

 
Always allow YouTube

 

 

Conclusion

The SAP Fiori Makers boot camp proved to be a pivotal step in badenovaNETZE’s digital transformation journey. By combining cutting-edge SAP technologies with a user-centric design approach, the team not only tackled complex operational challenges but also delivered a solution that significantly enhances efficiency and user satisfaction. The rapid progress made during the boot camp—cutting down development time by six months and projecting savings of 115 working days per year—underscores the power of collaboration and innovation.

With the project already underway and goals for 2025 set, badenovaNETZE is well-positioned to continue refining and optimizing processes with an even more engaged and satisfied workforce.

Make your voice heard by joining the SAP User Research Panel initiative. Registration is easy and taking part in research studies is convenient. To learn more about the SAP Fiori Makers boot camp, which includes a workshop following the design-led development process, visit our web page.

This story originally appeared on SAPinsider with the support of Suparna Chawla.

Have a nice day,
Albert
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Does Recruitment support auto-delegation of workflows?
entry.content:
Thank you Sanjeet! I did find Recruiting Subscriber Settings under Manage Recruiting Settings in our Prod environment, not in our UAT environment.
It seems I can assign in all cases to next level manager - would that be the next level manager of the roles indicated below? I.e. if assigned People & Culture person is unavailable, will it then be assigned to the manager of that PnC person?

And it will be only for the selected situation, e.g. approver is on time off?

entry.title: Change the default base directory for Enable Now content
entry.content:
Hello,

We are working in a WVM env to record S4 content. However, this env is shared and only parts of it set up as roaming profiles. What this means, is all sync'd SEN content needs to get re-sync'd every single time we start the VM.

I have identified a possible solution which would require changing the default local storage location (C:\Users\userid\AppData\Local\Temp\wpb_cloud) to a different mapped drive. However, this doesn't seem possible to do. Can anyone confirm that is the case?

Thx

entry.title: Permission Roles/Groups - Restrict access to entities to self
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm using the SAP SuccessFactors OData API to retrieve certain entities via a dedicated API user. However, I’m trying to figure out whether it’s possible to configure permission roles or groups in a way that lets me retrieve only the entities that are relevant to the end user (not the API user), based on specific business rules or dynamic criteria.

For example, take the Cost Center Foundation Object: I want to allow certain users to query cost centers, but only the ones where they are assigned as the cost center manager.

I know that in Business Rules, there is a way to check if a user is the currently logged-in user—like in this screenshot:

But I couldn't find any documentation or approach that allows something similar at the permission level. I'm not even sure if it's possible, but maybe someone has encountered a similar requirement and solved it with a different workaround (e.g. via MDF roles, custom filters, or proxy logic).

Any insight or experience would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Check compatibility pack usage in SAP S/4HANA with SAP EarlyWatch Alert
entry.content:
Hello Suzanne

My company is an SAP VAR partner, and we need to produce and send EWA reports to the client from our solution manager. However, with the new chapters only available through me.sap.com, we're forced to retrieve the reports manually.

Is it possible to send the new EWA reports in PDF or HTML (or both) format from me.sap.com to an email ?

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

entry.title: Re: Error 404 when trying to access a full stack CAP appl...
entry.content:
@MioYasutakeThank you for your response. Eventually, after posting this, I came across the below tutorial from SAP

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/deploy-to-cf..html

Here the steps mentioned helped me change the manifest.json as well as the mta.yaml file.

However, I do have one query. When the application is deployed, I see only 2 applications in my dev space, i.e. the service and the db-deployer.
Usually, we also have an approuter right from where we are able to access even the html5 app and accessing the html 5 app through cockpit leads to auth issue? What do we have to do do make our config that way? Also, is that the standard config which is followed?


Thanks,

Regards,

Raihan

entry.title: Re: Using Integration Suite with the power of AI
entry.content:
एहो

@1nbuc Thank you very much for sharing. Really looking forward to implement a test scenario and dig deeper!

entry.title: Re: Requesting Help ,Loading FILE From AL11 based on TIMESTAMP
entry.content:
Please check below two function modules.

1. /SDF/GET_FILE_INFO - It will give you list of all files in AL11 directory along with DATE and TIME. SORT it as per your requirement and find the workaround. 

2. EPS2_GET_DIRECTORY_LISITING - It will give you timestamp along with filename. SORT it as per your requirement and find the workaround.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Request for Hamburg - ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
entry.content:
Dear (former) SAP colleagues 😉,

We at ASCAVO would be delighted to host an SAP CodeJam this September in Hamburg, Germany.

Proposed Dates:
Preferably between September 25th and 30th, or alternatively during the first two weeks of September (September 4th to 12th).

Topic:
ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

We would be very grateful for the opportunity and would greatly look forward to welcoming the SAP CodeJam expert and fellow developers to Hamburg.

Cheers,
Max

entry.title: Re: Can we hide or disable Self-description (introductory...
entry.content:
A possible workaround is to use "Manage Languages" to replace the text of the labels in the pop-up screen.

The standard values are the ones below, and you can replace the texts referring your internal policies about that content in your organizations. 

PPX_Self_DescriptionSelf-Description
PPX_Self_Description_DescIntroduce yourself briefly:
PPX_Self_Description_PlaceholderWrite about your professional background, skills, and interests

entry.title: Our SAP environment (7.50) utilizes OpenText software via archive link to store attachments for vari
entry.content:
Our SAP environment (7.50) utilizes OpenText software via archive link to store attachments for various business requirements.  To date we have amassed a large volume of attachments resulting in an increase of storage area.  In an attempt to avoid continually adding disc space to the OpenText database has anyone employed or know of a mass deletion scheme using parameters such as date ranges to address their growing volume of attachments.  There doesn’t appear to be any transaction in SAP which allows for this. 

Does any one have an update to share?  Thanks and Regards.

entry.title: Re: Efficient Data Maintenance in SAP Analytics Cloud System Overview - Housekeeping
entry.content:
Hi Nikhil,

Even i had a similar question, how i convinced is once story or MA/DA created, we do execute for testing at least once hence they will appear with "Last Access date" in System overview. my assumption might be wrong and looking for if any alternative available.

entry.title: Re: Our SAP environment (7.50) utilizes OpenText software via archive link to store attachments for
entry.content: That can be managed with the retention period settings in the configuration.

entry.title: Re: Error FAA_POST214 when reversing Asset Document of Le...
entry.content: Hi, Following note 3224015, Last closed year is 2023 and opened year is 2025 so difference is 2 years and this is the reason error FAA_POST214 is appearing. System by no means allowing to post reversal unless and until one of the opened year is closed. In sequence i have to close 2024 which requires depreciation posting for all 12 periods of 2024. As I mentioned in question, no depreciation is posted yet. I am quite skeptic that posting depreciation will further complicate the original concern which is to reverse the Legacy transfer posting. Do you think there could be any safe workaround given the circumstances explained in question above?

entry.title: Re: How to create a OData Service in Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi iamnaveen,

The equivalent extensibility for such a custom table is probably the Custom Business Object, which you can expose as an OData service and create a UI with the CBO.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b45696ca0d9143cba040797e9c71aa44.html?locale=en-US

 If you want to adapt an app to use it or use it in your own app, you probably need to look into Developer Extensibility. I don't know a way of adding the table to an existing UI without Developer Extensibility if that is what you are trying to do. 

If you could describe your use case, there might be a better approach.

There are several blogs in the community about CBOs.

I hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Paging in a RAP service with complex search and a Fio...
entry.content:
This issue was eventually resolved for us. If you are on a S/4 HANA version lower than 2020, an adaptation project has a setting where you can increase it. This was the only solution that really worked for us (2020 version). Not sure if this is also on later version, but we found out it was gone in 2023.

 

Because we are now on 2023 since last Summer, we have moved away from this solution, because it is also not best practice to create a adaptation project for a custom app. Below code can be used to make it work then:

 

@ui: {
  headerInfo: { typeName: 'Application Name',
                typeNamePlural: 'Application Names',
                title: { type: #STANDARD,
                         label: 'Application Namer',
                         value: 'Application Name' }
               },
  presentationVariant: [{
     sortOrder: [{
        by: 'Article',
        direction:  #ASC
       }],
     maxItems: 100,
     visualizations : [{ type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
     }]
}

entry.title: Microsoft Project integratuion with BW/4HANA or SAC
entry.content:
Hello.

We want to know if It's possible to integrate Microsoft Project with our current BW/4HANA (2.0 SP07) or our SAC (client 2025.1.12 and server 2025.1.10).

Regards.

Marcelo Mazieiro.

entry.title: Re: How to Call RESTful APIs from SAP ABAP: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content: @S_Shukla No need for that, as far as I know. You perhaps do need to register the certificate if it is a secured URL.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation - changes?!
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has gone through updates in Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation (FCV) configuration, especially in Manage Your Solution / CBC > SSCUI 105450 (Define Rules for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation).

Let’s clarify the changes and how to work with them now.

 

What Changed in SSCUI 105450?

Previously, you could directly assign G/L accounts for realized and unrealized foreign currency differences. In newer releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public, the configuration interface has been updated, and some fields like "Realized Gain/Loss" have been removed or renamed.

You now see something like this:

Account Type

What it Means

Unrealized Gain/Loss

Still used for FC valuation postings (standard)

Reset Gain/Loss

New term introduced for resetting prior FC valuation entries

Valuation Offset

Technical offset account (e.g., balance sheet account)

 

What is the "Reset" account?

The Reset Gain/Loss Account is used for reversing or resetting previously posted unrealized foreign currency valuation documents. This is part of the reset step in FCV processing — typically when the next valuation cycle begins or at period-end closing.

  • In Advanced FCV, the reset run reverses the prior period’s valuation entries (created with unrealized accounts).
  • The reset postings now go to a separate "Reset" G/L account, not re-using the original unrealized accounts.
  • This separation is designed for audit transparency and clearer reporting.

 

Where are realized Gain/Loss accounts now?

In the Advanced FCV, realized FX differences (e.g., at invoice clearing) are no longer configured via SSCUI 105450.

Instead:

  • Realized Gain/Loss G/L accounts are configured under Financial Accounting > General Ledger > Business Transactions > Gain/Loss Realized Exchange Differences.
  • Or they are automatically derived via Account Determination for Financial Accounting.

In short: Realized gain/loss accounts are handled by standard postings (e.g., at clearing of open items), not by Advanced FCV.

 

Where to find the documentation?

Here are the best references:

1. SAP Help Portal – Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD

Search: “Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation”
(Look for app-specific documentation for Schedule FCV Jobs, Valuation Runs, etc

2. Configuration Help in CBC
In SSCUI 105450, click the “i” (info icon) or the documentation link in CBC to access SAP’s embedded documentation.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy
